004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http.header;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025/**
026 * Represents an HTTP <c>Range</c> header.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * Request only part of an entity. Bytes are numbered from 0.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
033 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
034 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
035 * (and possibly earlier).
036 *
037 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
038 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
039 * </ul>
040 *
041 * @since 9.2.1
042 */
043public class Range extends HttpStringHeader {
044
045   /** The header name */
046   public static final String NAME = "Range";
047
048   /**
049    * Constructor with an eager value.
050    *
051    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
052    */
053   public Range(String value) {
054      super(NAME, value);
055   }
056
057   /**
058    * Constructor with a lazy value supplier.
059    *
060    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
061    */
062   public Range(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
063      super(NAME, valueSupplier);
064   }
065
066   /**
067    * Static factory method with an eager value.
068    *
069    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
070    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
071    */
072   public static Range of(String value) {
073      return new Range(value);
074   }
075
076   /**
077    * Static factory method with a lazy value supplier.
078    *
079    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
080    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
081    */
082   public static Range of(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
083      return new Range(valueSupplier);
084   }
085
086   /**
087    * Creates a new {@link Builder} defaulting to the <c>bytes</c> range unit.
088    *
089    * @return A new builder. Never <jk>null</jk>.
090    */
091   public static Builder create() {
092      return new Builder();
093   }
094
095   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
096   // Inner types
097   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
098
099   /**
100    * Fluent builder for assembling an HTTP <c>Range</c> header value.
101    *
102    * <p>
103    * Composes the range unit (default <c>bytes</c>) and one or more range specs defined by
104    * <a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7233#section-3.1'>RFC 7233 §3.1</a> into the
105    * <c>unit=spec,spec,...</c> wire format.  Typed setters cover the three byte-range-spec forms — a closed range
106    * (<c>0-499</c>) via {@link #range(long, long)}, an open-ended range (<c>500-</c>) via {@link #from(long)}, and a
107    * suffix range (<c>-500</c>) via {@link #suffix(long)} — and {@link #spec(String)} is a generic escape hatch.
108    *
109    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
110    * <p class='bjava'>
111    *    String <jv>value</jv> = Range.<jsm>create</jsm>()
112    *       .range(0, 499)
113    *       .from(9500)
114    *       .build();
115    *    <jc>// =&gt; "bytes=0-499,9500-"</jc>
116    * </p>
117    *
118    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
119    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Range}
120    *    <li class='extlink'><a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7233'>RFC 7233 - HTTP/1.1 Range Requests</a>
121    * </ul>
122    *
123    * @since 10.0.0
124    */
125   public static class Builder {
126
127      private String unit = "bytes";
128      private final List<String> specs = new ArrayList<>();
129
130      /**
131       * Sets the range unit (default <c>bytes</c>).
132       *
133       * @param value The range unit. Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
134       * @return This object.
135       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
136       */
137      public Builder unit(String value) {
138         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
139         var v = value.trim();
140         if (v.isEmpty())
141            throw iaex("range unit must not be blank");
142         unit = v;
143         return this;
144      }
145
146      /**
147       * Adds a closed byte-range spec of the form <c>start-end</c>.
148       *
149       * @param startValue The first byte position (inclusive). Must be non-negative.
150       * @param endValue The last byte position (inclusive). Must be greater than or equal to {@code startValue}.
151       * @return This object.
152       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the positions are negative or out of order.
153       */
154      public Builder range(long startValue, long endValue) {
155         if (startValue < 0)
156            throw iaex("range start must be non-negative: {0}", startValue);
157         if (endValue < startValue)
158            throw iaex("range end {0} must be >= start {1}", endValue, startValue);
159         return spec(startValue + "-" + endValue);
160      }
161
162      /**
163       * Adds an open-ended byte-range spec of the form <c>start-</c> (from the position to the end).
164       *
165       * @param startValue The first byte position (inclusive). Must be non-negative.
166       * @return This object.
167       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code startValue} is negative.
168       */
169      public Builder from(long startValue) {
170         if (startValue < 0)
171            throw iaex("range start must be non-negative: {0}", startValue);
172         return spec(startValue + "-");
173      }
174
175      /**
176       * Adds a suffix byte-range spec of the form <c>-length</c> (the final {@code length} bytes).
177       *
178       * @param length The number of trailing bytes. Must be non-negative.
179       * @return This object.
180       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code length} is negative.
181       */
182      public Builder suffix(long length) {
183         if (length < 0)
184            throw iaex("suffix length must be non-negative: {0}", length);
185         return spec("-" + length);
186      }
187
188      /**
189       * Generic escape hatch for adding any range spec verbatim.
190       *
191       * @param value The range spec (e.g. <js>"0-499"</js>). Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
192       * @return This object.
193       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
194       */
195      public Builder spec(String value) {
196         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
197         var v = value.trim();
198         if (v.isEmpty())
199            throw iaex("range spec must not be blank");
200         specs.add(v);
201         return this;
202      }
203
204      /**
205       * Builds the rendered <c>Range</c> header value.
206       *
207       * <p>
208       * Returns an empty string when no range specs have been registered.
209       *
210       * @return The header value. Never <jk>null</jk>.
211       */
212      public String build() {
213         if (specs.isEmpty())
214            return "";
215         return unit + "=" + String.join(",", specs);
216      }
217
218      /**
219       * Builds a {@link Range} header bean directly from this builder.
220       *
221       * @return A {@link Range} carrying {@link #build()}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
222       */
223      public Range toHeader() {
224         return Range.of(build());
225      }
226
227      @Override
228      public String toString() {
229         return build();
230      }
231   }
232}
